summary: Re-linked the lb library by updating configure.py object classifications and making small source/header/include fixes needed for lb files to participate in the normal link. The largest functional source addition was in lbanim.c: it introduced a partial C implementation around HSD_AObj/HSD_FObj/HSD_JObj handling, while the remaining lbanim assembly was reformatted/regenerated as relocatable-style asm with .fn/.endfn labels and local offset labels. No PR body or review comments were available, so rationale is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Updated configure.py lb object statuses: lbcollision, lb_00B0, lbarchive, lbanim, and lbrefract moved from Unknown to NonMatching; lbvector and lbfile moved from Unknown to Matching.

Reformatted asm/melee/lb/lbanim.s to use `.file "lbanim.c"`, `.text`, `.balign 4`, `.fn ... global`, `.endfn`, and local offset-style labels such as `.L_00000024` instead of address-derived `.L_8001...` labels.

Kept lbanim assembly coverage for lbAnim_8001E560, an internal/newly globalized `fn_8001E60C`, lbAnim_8001E6D8, and lbAnim_8001E7E8, with call sites adjusted to use local relocatable branch placeholders in the asm excerpt.
Expanded src/melee/lb/lbanim.c from extern-only declarations into real C code using HSD_FObj, HSD_AObj, and HSD_JObj types.

Added a static `getFObj(s32, s8)` declaration for the function at 01E560 and implemented `lbAnim_8001E6D8` in C, including AObj removal/allocation, setting flags/rewind/end frame/FObj, assigning `jobj->aobj`, and setting or clearing JObj flag 8.
Added helper `foo(HSD_JObj*)` in lbanim.c to scan an AObj FObj linked list and move the first FObj whose `obj_type` is 12 to the head of the list.

Changed lbanim.h include guard from `melee_lb_lbanim_h` to address-style `GALE01_01E560`, added baselib forward declarations and placeholder include, and exported prototypes for `lbAnim_8001E60C` and `lbAnim_8001E7E8` using `UNK_T`.
Narrowed OS includes in lbarchive.c and lbfile.c from `<dolphin/os.h>` to `<dolphin/os/OSError.h>`.
Removed MUST_MATCH guards around the `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` wrapping `lbFile_800161A0`, making the dont_inline pragma unconditional.
Adjusted lb_00B0.c include hygiene by adding `IWYU pragma: keep` to required includes and removing several now-unneeded baselib includes.
smart_moves: The relink did not simply flip configure statuses; it paired status changes with targeted include and pragma fixes so the lb objects could be built in their new categories.
Using a small wrapper/helper in lbanim.c for the FObj linked-list reorder isolates a recognizable behavior from the larger animation setup routine and makes the partial decomp easier to reason about.
Keeping `getFObj` static while exposing only still-needed public/unknown prototypes in lbanim.h avoids leaking an internal helper into the header.
Switching broad `<dolphin/os.h>` includes to `<dolphin/os/OSError.h>` reduces dependency surface and can prevent accidental type/prototype availability from masking missing includes.
The asm regeneration with `.fn`/`.endfn` and offset labels makes the remaining assembly more compatible with linked object workflows and less tied to absolute address labels.
decomp_lessons: When re-linking a library, expect a chain of small build hygiene fixes: configure object statuses, header guards/prototypes, include specificity, and pragmas may all need to line up together.
Object status changes in configure.py should reflect actual build/link behavior: this PR marks some lb units Matching, some NonMatching, rather than treating all formerly Unknown files uniformly.

For partially decompiled files, keep remaining asm symbols and C declarations synchronized. Here, lbanim.c declares a static helper corresponding to 01E560 while the header exposes only the unresolved external-facing routines.
Header cleanup can be part of matching work: adding forward includes and placeholder types let prototypes exist without over-including full baselib definitions.
Use unconditional pragmas if the matching/build behavior depends on them outside MUST_MATCH configurations; lbFile_800161A0 kept `dont_inline` active regardless of MUST_MATCH.
Relocatable-style asm labels and `.fn` blocks are useful when a file is being re-linked, because absolute-address labels can become awkward once the assembler/linker needs normal symbol boundaries.
naming_conventions: Address-based include guards appear in this slice as `GALE01_01E560` for lbanim.h.
Unidentified public functions retain address-style names such as `lbAnim_8001E60C` and `lbAnim_8001E7E8`.
An internal assembly/C helper at 01E560 is named descriptively in C as `getFObj` while the asm symbol still appears as `lbAnim_8001E560` in the diff excerpt.
A still-unknown internal asm symbol is named `fn_8001E60C` in regenerated assembly.
Temporary or placeholder C names remain weak in this PR: the struct is named `asdf` and the helper is named `foo`, so future cleanup should not treat those as final semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Regenerate remaining lbanim assembly with `.file`, `.text`, `.balign 4`, `.fn`, and `.endfn` annotations for cleaner object boundaries.
Use local offset labels like `.L_00000150` instead of absolute address labels when converting asm into a form suitable for re-linking.
Preserve exact low-level behavior in partial C by manipulating HSD_FObj linked-list pointers directly rather than abstracting the operation away.
Use an explicit unused stack array (`u32 unused[2]`) in `lbAnim_8001E6D8`, likely to preserve stack layout/register allocation for matching; exact motivation is inferred from the diff.
Keep `#pragma dont_inline` around small functions such as `lbFile_800161A0` when inlining would alter object matching or link behavior.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `foo` or `asdf` in src/melee/lb/lbanim.c.
Find later PRs that fully decompile `getFObj`, `lbAnim_8001E60C`, or `lbAnim_8001E7E8`.
Check whether the configure.py lb object statuses changed again after PR 1148.
Search for other relink PRs that replace `<dolphin/os.h>` with narrower Dolphin OS headers.
Search for other uses of address-style include guards like `GALE01_01E560`.
